business.headline: Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

Jio IPO moves ahead as Reliance bets big on AI and new energyRIL AGM 2026: Jio Platforms to file IPO papers with Sebi; Mukesh Ambani unveils five major value creation pathways, bets big on AI, energy self-reliance, satellite connectivity, media, FMCG and global expansion

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

entertainment.headline: India's Got Latent 2 X review: Upset fans ask Samay Raina, 'Where's the old magic?' Samay Raina's India's Got Latent Season 2 has received mixed reactions after its premiere with Alia Bhatt and Sharvari. While some viewers enjoyed parts of the episode, many felt the show had lost the raw, unfiltered humour that made the first season a hit.

Shraddha Kapoor's brother can't hold back tears after Eetha teaser: My jaan sister Actor Shraddha Kapoor's brother Siddhanth shared an emotional reaction after watching the teaser of her upcoming film Eetha. His message came as the first glimpse of the film drew praise online for Shraddha's transformation.

Why Barun Sobti's rare home tour with Farah Khan has viewers divided Actor Barun Sobti's appearance on Farah Khan's cooking vlog set off a split online reaction. While some viewers questioned his behaviour, fans said fatigue after months of shooting explained it.

Samantha, 39, is pregnant. Actor to welcome first child with Raj Nidimoru this year Actor Samantha is set to welcome her first child with filmmaker Raj Nidimoru later this year. The news comes after fans speculated about her pregnancy during the celebrations for Maa Inti Bangaaram.

Our precious little one: Ram Charan, Upasana reveal daughter Klin Kaara's face Actor Ram Charan and his wife, entrepreneur Upasana Kamineni Konidela, revealed the face of their daughter Klin Kaara on Saturday. The couple shared a family photo with a heartfelt note to mark Klin's birthday.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram celebration with Raj Nidimoru sparks pregnancy buzz Actor Samantha celebrated the positive response to Maa Inti Bangaaram with filmmaker Raj Nidimoru on Saturday. As videos of the celebration surfaced online, fans began speculating that the couple might be expecting their first child.

Aamir Khan shares details of his private ceremony with Gauri Spratt Aamir Khan said he and Gauri Spratt will have a simple registered wedding at home on July 5. The ceremony will remain a private family affair, reflecting the couple's wish to keep it basic.

1920 Cold Winter: Vikram Bhatt announces new horror film after Haunted 3D Following the success of Haunted 3D, filmmaker Vikram Bhatt has announced the next chapter in his popular horror franchise, 1920: Cold Winter. The makers also unveiled the film's first-look poster.

Actor Samantha thrilled by Maa Inti Bangaaram's opening, praises female audience Actor Samantha thanked audiences after her latest release, Maa Inti Bangaaram, grossed Rs 13.15 crore worldwide on its opening day.

Actor Rukmini Vasanth deepfake row: Bengaluru cyber police arrests 3 accused Bengaluru Cyber Crime Police have arrested three accused for allegedly creating and sharing AI-generated visuals of actor Rukmini Vasanth.

Alpha duo Alia Bhatt, Sharvari to kick off Samay Raina's Latent 2 premiere episode Actors Alia Bhatt and Sharvari Wagh will appear as special guests on the first episode of Samay Raina's India's Got Latent Season 2. The episode will premiere today, June 20 at 7 pm on Netflix and YouTube.

Watch: SRK lights up Supriya Sule's daughter's pre-wedding bash with his hit moves Actor Shah Rukh Khan was among the other guests at politician Supriya Sule's daughter, Revati and Sarang Lakhani's pre-wedding celebration. Several videos of the superstar's dance have emerged online.

Priyanka Chopra confirms new project with Angelina Jolie, keeps details under wraps Actor Priyanka Chopra has said she is set to work with Hollywood star Angelina Jolie on a new project. Chopra remained tight-lipped about the project and refrained from sharing any information about the same.

Comic Zakir Khan watches in awe, bows and hugs Johnny Lever at Papa Yaar show Veteran actor Johnny Lever made a surprise appearance at comedian Zakir Khan's Papa Yaar show in Mumbai and joined him on stage. The comedian later thanked the veteran actor with a special post on social media.

How TVF cracked the code of middle-class storytelling TVF built its success by telling honest, relatable stories of middle-class India instead of chasing escapist drama. In this weeks' Cinematic Saturday TVF founder Arunabh Kumar, actors, Namita Dubey and Sunny Hinduja, and writer Nitin Tiwari talk about how TVF shows made ordinary lives feel extraordinary.

Friends director James Burrows dies at 85, cast honours sitcom legend James Burrows, Friends director and Cheers co-creator, has died at 85. The cast of the popular sitcom paid heartfelt tributes and recalled his influence on their work and lives.

Maa Inti Bangaaram box office Day 1: Samantha film registers Rs 10.7 crore globally Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram opened with Rs 10.70 crore worldwide on Day 1. The Telugu market drove the start, with occupancy rising sharply through the day.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 1: Shahid, Rashmika, Kriti's film records solid opening Shahid Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon's Cocktail 2 opened to a solid start on June 19. The film earned Rs 13.50 crore nett in India on its opening day.

Ameesha Patel says Gadar set an impossible benchmark: Not every film can be iconic Ameesha Patel reflected on Gadar's enduring pull and why audiences still return to Sakina. She said iconic films fix actors in public memory and set near-impossible expectations for later work.

general news.headline: Admiration for PM has gone beyond physical world: Congress mocks TharoorCongress took a swipe at Shashi Tharoor over his remarks on PM Modi and Indian sailors, with Tharoor clarifying he was citing published reports while Pawan Khera took a swipe saying he now seemed "capable of hearing what Modi doesn't even say."

Iran closes Hormuz again over Israel's Lebanon strikes, Vance says it's still openTehran announces closure of the Strait of Hormuz and warns of further measures, while the US disputes reports of any shipping blockade in one of the world's most vital maritime routes.

NEET re-test: 22 lakh aspirants, 1.38 lakh CCTVs, 51,000 jammers in zero-error pushThe NTA says it is ready to conduct NEET UG 2026 with extensive security across centres. The measures aim to ensure fairness, prevent rumours and ease exam-day stress for candidates.

My popularity is none of your concern: Meloni jabs Trump again over begged pic claimUS President Donald Trump reignited a diplomatic row by claiming Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni repeatedly sought photographs with him at the G7 Summit and was trying to boost her popularity, while also criticising Italy's stance on US military operations and Iran.

The Great IIT Reversal: Why are toppers choosing Civil Engineering over AI?Handcrafted ByOur Editors For years, the IIT dream meant Computer Science. But as AI reshapes careers and India's infrastructure boom gathers pace, some of the country's top JEE Advanced rankers are taking a fresh look at Civil Engineering. Their choices may signal a shift in the future of engineering careers.

GER 0-1 CIV, Live: Cote d'Ivoire start strong in 2nd halfGermany (GER) vs Ivory Coast (CIV), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live score and updates: Ivory Coast come close to doubling their lead as Franck Kessie's shot is blocked after a slick one-two with Amad Diallo in the 50th minute.

No food, no water: Abhijeet Dipke accuses police of cracking down on CJP protestAt Jantar Mantar, Abhijeet Dipke alleged that police restricted facilities and entry during the student protest. He said the agitation would continue peacefully despite fears of detention and growing questions over the curbs.

TMC's Rs 440-crore bank accounts frozen after rebel MLAs seek probePolice have frozen debit operations in three TMC bank accounts after complaints from rebel MLAs. The move deepens the party's internal power struggle by bringing its finances under criminal scrutiny.

Samantha, 39, is pregnant. Actor to welcome first child with Raj Nidimoru this yearActor Samantha is set to welcome her first child with filmmaker Raj Nidimoru later this year. The news comes after fans speculated about her pregnancy during the celebrations for Maa Inti Bangaaram.

Absurd remarks: Govt slams Pak President's comments on religious sites in IndiaThe Ministry of External Affairs was responding to a statement by the Pakistan President, in which he expressed "deep concern" over alleged threats to historic Muslim religious sites in India.

Couldn't clear NEET many times: Aspirant dies by suicide 2 days before re-examA 22-year-old medical aspirant, Jatin Kumar, was found dead at his Ghaziabad home two days before the NEET-UG exam. In a video recorded shortly before his death, he said no one should be held responsible and referred to his repeated inability to clear the examination.

6 killed, 18 pilgrims injured after pillar collapses at Maharashtra templeSix people were killed and 18 others injured after a stone pillar of an under-construction sabha mandap collapsed at the Yashwadi Hanuman Temple in Maharashtra's Parbhani district on Saturday, trapping devotees under the debris.

Indians want to sell old phones, but nearly 70 per cent don't. Here's whyA fresh survey of 8,000 Indians reveals that nearly 70 per cent hold back from selling their old smartphones due to data privacy concerns. With most people relying on factory resets, which may not fully wipe data. In other words, trust remains the biggest challenge in India's growing phone resale market.

Flood and furnace: South and Northeast India to see rain Sunday, North to sizzleOn Sunday, June 21, heavy rain will soak the northeast and the southern peninsula while a heatwave will grip the northern and central plains. The IMD says the stalled monsoon should push forward around June 23.

Indian firm reveals when it will launch country's 1st private communication satelliteAnanth Technologies is building AnanthSat-1, India's first privately made communication satellite. Chairman Dr Subba Rao Pavuluri told IndiaToday.in it will carry more than 100 Gbps to widen broadband, tele-education and telemedicine across remote India.

Video: Uttarakhand cop, 38, collapses during workout at gym, diesIn Uttarakhand's Champawat, SOG officer Girish Bhatt collapsed while working out at a gym and was declared dead at a hospital. His death has prompted widespread grief and fresh discussion around sudden medical emergencies during intense exercise.

What an Indian paranormal investigator learnt after a decade of chasing ghost storiesParanormal investigator Sarbajeet Mohanty explains how his team tests haunting claims through scientific methods. He says most cases have natural explanations, while folklore and fear often fill the gaps.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Watch: PM wishes President Murmu at her native village in Odisha on 68th birthdayPrime Minister Narendra Modi has extended warm birthday greetings to President Droupadi Murmu. The Prime Minister said that her journey, marked by courage, simplicity, humility and unwavering commitment to public service, continues to inspire people across the country.

Chaos in Dhaka as Hindus hold massive protest, raise Jai Shri Ram chantsThousands of Hindus marched in Dhaka protesting the alleged desecration of a Lord Ram image and the halting of a statue project. The protests renewed focus on minority rights concerns in Muslim-majority Bangladesh.

How TVF cracked the code of middle-class storytellingTVF built its success by telling honest, relatable stories of middle-class India instead of chasing escapist drama. In this weeks' Cinematic Saturday TVF founder Arunabh Kumar, actors, Namita Dubey and Sunny Hinduja, and writer Nitin Tiwari talk about how TVF shows made ordinary lives feel extraordinary.

Monsoon rain: Clouds to begin gaining strength after weeks of deep sleepThe southwest monsoon is showing signs of revival, with rain clouds strengthening over Central India. The shift could ease rainfall deficits and support kharif sowing as thunderstorms build across the region.

TVK ally IUML ends 60-year alliance with DMK, backs Vijay's party for local pollsIUML has quit the DMK-led alliance in Tamil Nadu and joined Vijay's TVK for the local body polls. The shift ends a nearly six-decade partnership and resets alliance equations ahead of the elections.

Jaiswal steals Rohit's thunder as India rout Afghanistan 3-0 in ODI seriesYashasvi Jaiswal hit a splendid 110* in the final match of India's ODI series against Afghanistan. Jaiswal's innings stole the thunder from two terrific performances on the day - first by Prasidh Krishna, and then by Rohit Sharma.

sports.headline: Beyond the game: The three women making history at the FIFA World Cup 2026 Tori Penso, Brooke Mayo and Kathryn Nesbitt are breaking barriers at FIFA World Cup 2026 as the tournament's only all-women officiating crew.

Brazil President mocks Neymar's World Cup absence with 'Work-From-Home' jibe FIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil President Lula joked that Neymar is the national team's first "work-from-home" player as the injured star continues his recovery and pushes to return for Brazil's World Cup match against Scotland.

Ronald Koeman's Dutch answer critics in style with five-star rout of Sweden NED vs SWE, FIFA World Cup 2026: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored twice each as the Netherlands thrashed Sweden 5-1 in Houston. Crysencio Summerville added a late fifth as Ronald Koeman's side climbed atop Group F.

India will miss Shreyanka Patil, but Prema Rawat is equally exciting: India bowling coach Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: India bowling coach Aavishkar Salvi said that India will miss Shreyanka Patil, who has been ruled out due to a foot injury, but he hailed young leg-spinner Prema Rawat as an equally exciting prospect.

Aryan Nehra creates history in 800m freestyle, qualifies for CWG, Asian Games Aryan Nehra became the first Indian to swim the men's 800m freestyle in under eight minutes at the National Aquatics Championships. The 7:59.36 effort secured Commonwealth and Asian Games qualification and underlined a strong week for Indian swimming.

Neeraj Chopra and Parul Chaudhary win top honours at Indian Athletics Awards Neeraj Chopra and Parul Chaudhary bagged the top honours at the inaugural Indian Athletics Awards, while legendary sprinter P T Usha was recognised with the Lifetime Achievement Award.

Has Jaiswal done enough to earn England ODI call-up? Shubman Gill reveals IND vs AFG, 3rd ODI: Yashasvi Jaiswal was sensational in the third ODI on Saturday at the Chepauk, helping India win the series 3-0. But will the knock be good enough to earn him a ticket to England?

Pakistan Women face early exit threat after batting shocker vs Bangladesh Women's T20 World Cup, PAK vs BAN: Pakistan are in danger of making an early exit from the tournament after losing to Nigar Sultana Joty's Bangladesh by 20 runs on Saturday at the Rose Bowl in Southampton.

Tilak Varma breaks silence on ugly Super Over spat on Tri-Nation tour India A captain Tilak Varma addressed the controversial Super Over defeat to Sri Lanka A for the first time, saying the team only questioned continuing play under fading light and had no intention of creating unnecessary arguments with officials.

GER 0-1 CIV, Live: Kessie keeps Germany on back foot Germany (GER) vs Ivory Coast (CIV), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live score and updates: Captain Franck Kessie's goal in the first half puts Ivory Coast ahead at the end of the first half. Can Germany come back or will the latter secure a upset win.

Aryna Sabalenka rocked with bagel, slumps to nightmare defeat before WimbledonWorld No.1 Aryna Sabalenka suffered a major blow ahead of Wimbledon after a 6-0 final-set collapse against Jessica Pegula in the Berlin Open semifinals. The defeat continues a worrying run of form for the Belarusian.

India bank on South Africa tour lessons to turn tables in Women's T20 World CupWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: India bowling coach Aavishkar Salvi said that India learned a lot from their 1-4 defeat in South Africa and will be looking to use the learnings in their upcoming clash on Sunday.

Jaiswal steals Rohit's thunder as India rout Afghanistan 3-0 in ODI seriesYashasvi Jaiswal hit a splendid 110* in the final match of India's ODI series against Afghanistan. Jaiswal's innings stole the thunder from two terrific performances on the day - first by Prasidh Krishna, and then by Rohit Sharma.

Shreyanka Patil determined to bounce back after injury setback: I will fly againShreyanka Patil has expressed deep disappointment after being ruled out of the Women's T20 World Cup due to a foot injury. She also said she is determined to make a comeback and thanked fans for their concern and support.

Australia Women blow away Netherlands, build pressure on India in semis raceWomen's T20 World Cup, AUS vs NED: Australia have one foot in the door of the semi-finals after beating the Netherlands by 98 runs on Saturday at the Rose Bowl in Southampton. With seemingly one spot still up for grabs, India will need to be cautious heading into their match against South Africa.

Indian football is in chaos. AIFF's latest move is changing its nameThe AIFF has approved a proposal to rename itself the Football Federation of Bharat, subject to clearances. The move comes as the federation is still working through the Indian Super League's future structure.

5 runs gift, 2 no balls, 2 wides: Afghanistan hit bottom in ODI series vs IndiaIND vs AFG, 3rd ODI: Afghanistan endured a tough outing on Saturday at the Chepauk after they were penalised five runs and Azmatullah Omarzai bowled a nightmare first over where he leaked 23 runs.

Chepauk roars for Prasidh Krishna as fast bowler stakes claim for World Cup 2027Prasidh Krishna produced a devastating spell in the Chennai heat to register his maiden ODI five-wicket haul against Afghanistan. This should strengthen his case for India's plans leading into the ODI World Cup 2027.

Smriti Mandhana, Shafali Verma brace for trial by fire against South Africa pacersVeteran pacer Shikha Pandey believes India openers Smriti Mandhana and Shafali Verma will face their toughest challenge of the Women's T20 World Cup against South Africa's formidable pace attack led by Shabnim Ismail and Marizanne Kapp.

New Zealand Women refuse to panic as faith in their process finally pays offWomen's T20 World Cup, NZ vs IRE: New Zealand found themselves in deep trouble after their batters came out firing in the powerplay but lost wickets in quick succession. However, the White Ferns showed plenty of character to recover from the early collapse and eventually edged past Ireland by four runs in a tense contest in Southampton on Friday.

F1 to return to India? Sports Minister to set up task force for revival of sportSports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya has ordered a task force to assess major motorsport events and map Formula One's return to India. The move fixes a 2028 target for a Grand Prix while linking the bid to investment, tourism and grassroots development.

IND vs AFG: Nitish Reddy returns for final ODI after recovering from thigh injuryNitish Kumar Reddy returned for the final ODI against Afghanistan after recovering from a thigh injury. India, already 2-0 up, also brought back Prasidh Krishna and Harsh Dubey to test depth.

Virat Kohli's England ODI return remains on track after positive injury updateVirat Kohli is recovering well from his IPL hamstring injury and will return to the BCCI's Centre of Excellence on June 22 for re-evaluation. The update keeps India hopeful before England and the wider World Cup build-up.

3rd ODI Highlights: Yashasvi Jaiswal 110* powers India to 3-0 series winIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) 3rd ODI Highlights: Yashasvi Jaiswal scored an unbeaten 110 as India beat Afghanistan by nine wickets at the Chepauk to win the three-match series 3-0.

Hardik Pandya ruled out of England ODIs, needs more time to regain fitnessIndia all-rounder Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of the upcoming ODI series against England after failing to attain the required fitness levels for 50-over cricket.

Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi be in the firing line after Sri Lanka A altercation?Sri Lanka A have played down talk of targeting Vaibhav Sooryavanshi before Sunday's Tri-Nation final against India A. The remarks frame the decider as a test of execution and temperament after their earlier Super Over flashpoint.

Asia is thriving at the FIFA World Cup. How far away are India?FIFA World Cup 2026: Asia's impressive World Cup start has reignited a familiar debate in India. But perhaps the bigger concern is not why India missed a 48-team World Cup, but why they could not qualify for a 24-team Asian Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
